description_long: This object consists of three rustic, cylindrical wooden barrels or buckets, each constructed from vertical wooden staves bound by two dark metal hoops—one near the top and one near the bottom. The wood appears weathered, with a natural grain and slightly uneven coloration, suggesting age and outdoor exposure. The interiors are visible and unfinished, matching the rough, utilitarian exterior. One of the barrels is tipped on its side, revealing a flat, circular wooden base, while the others stand upright and open. There are no visible handles, spouts, or decorative elements, emphasizing a simple, functional design. The barrels are likely used for storage, carrying, or collecting materials in urban or garden settings.
description: A set of three weathered wooden barrels or buckets, bound with dark metal hoops and open at the top, showing a rustic and utilitarian design.
description_view_0: Front view showing two upright open barrels and one barrel lying on its side, displaying the flat wooden base.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the side profile of the barrels, with the tipped barrel's circular base partially visible.
description_view_2: Rear view with all three barrels visible, emphasizing the open interiors and the arrangement of the metal hoops.
description_view_3: Side view focusing on the open tops and the cylindrical shape, with the tipped barrel's side and base in clear view.
